questions: websocket ki real-time apps er jonno?
answers: WebSocket is a communication protocol that provides full-duplex, bidirectional communication between client and server over a single TCP connection. It's perfect for real-time applications.

Unlike HTTP where the client always initiates requests, WebSocket creates a persistent connection where both client and server can send messages at any time. This makes it ideal for chat apps, live feeds, and gaming.
WebSocket starts with an HTTP handshake then upgrades to a persistent connection. This eliminates the overhead of repeated HTTP requests, enabling real-time data flow with minimal latency.

Use WebSocket for chat applications, live notifications, collaborative editing, stock tickers, and multiplayer games. Libraries like Socket.IO, ws for Node.js, and Django Channels make implementation straightforward.
